title: Are private credit troubles here to stay as firms cap withdrawals?
summary: Investors are growing increasingly concerned about the state of the private credit industry as Morgan Stanley (MS) and BlackRock (BLK) join the ranks of firms restricting withdrawals from their funds. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Bullseye American Ingenuity Fund portfolio manager Adam Johnson discuss the themes of uncertainty emerging from the private credit industry. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

title: How Weaker Execution And CEO Change Are Shifting The TIC Solutions (TIC) Investment Narrative
summary: TIC Solutions’ fair value estimate has been cut from $13.92 to $12.42 per share, a modest reduction that still matters if you are tracking where analysts think the stock should trade over time. The shift lines up with more cautious analyst views after weaker execution, Q4 earnings disappointment, and questions around leadership changes and integration progress, even as some bulls still point to attractive end markets and recurring revenue. title: JPMorgan, Partners Group Cancel UAE Events as War Drags On
summary: Partners Group has moved its annual global gathering of investors to Switzerland from Abu Dhabi, people familiar with the matter said, declining to be identified discussing the matter.  JPMorgan is re-scheduling its MENA Global Opportunities Conference, an invitation-only event for institutional investors, corporate executives, and financial sponsors, other people said.

title: Concorde Financial Exits Howard Hughes Holdings, Developer of Large Sun Belt Communities
summary: Howard Hughes Holdings owns large land holdings in fast-growing markets such as Las Vegas, Houston, and Phoenix, where it has developed master-planned communities over many years. Investors will watch whether the company can convert housing demand into both land sales and long-term retail, office, and mixed-use development.

title: Higher Oil Prices Could Bring a Global Economic Shock. This Is the Number to Watch.
summary: Oil prices are key to watch.  If they rise above $125 a barrel and stay there, it would create the type of economic ripples seen in other shocks, according to  JP Morgan  Chief Economist Bruce Kasman.  In the past five energy shocks, the last of which was when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, Brent has soared 50% above its trailing two-year average, according to Kasman.

summary: Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares have had a rough stretch heading into this week’s earnings. title: DB Stock Falls 7% After Revealing Nearly $30B Private Credit Exposure
summary: Deutsche Bank shares drop after revealing rise in private credit exposure, highlighting rising investor concern as scrutiny grows across the market.
